original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Recent-onset chest pain of suspected cardiac origin: assessment and diagnosis
original_registry_entry.population: Adults with recent-onset acute or stable chest pain or discomfort suspected to be cardiac in origin.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Initial, urgent, primary, outpatient, and specialist assessment of recent-onset chest pain.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for clinician-documented chest-pain history, associated symptoms, risk context, examination and reviewed tests, information, and safety-net discussion. It is old and only partial for atypical or established non-acute follow-up; diagnosis, urgency, testing, treatment, and referral decisions are not generated.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1.1-1.1.1.9 - information, uncertainty, test discussion, and recurrent-pain advice
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.

exact_sections.heading: Section 1.2 - acute chest-pain history, associated features, examination, ECG, and test context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting pain timing and characteristics, associated symptoms, cardiovascular history and risk, vital signs and examination, and clinician-reviewed ECG or test context; urgent conclusions remain clinician-led.

exact_sections.heading: Section 1.3 - stable chest-pain assessment and investigation context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documented stable-pain characteristics, risk and prior-investigation context, and clinician-selected or reviewed investigation discussion without automatic testing or diagnosis.

title: Recent-onset chest pain of suspected cardiac origin: assessment and diagnosis
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
population: Adults with recent-onset acute or stable chest pain or discomfort suspected to be cardiac in origin.
intended_setting: Initial, urgent, primary, outpatient, and specialist assessment of recent-onset chest pain.
